content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 1, 1988

C. L. Richmond, Foreman
San Luis Obispo County Grand Jury
County Government Center
San Luis Obispo, CA 93408

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-88-201

Dear Mr. Richmond:

You have written seeking an opinion concerning Arroyo
Grande Mayor Howard Mankins' duties under the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

The Commission's Legal Division does provide written advice
to persons with questions about their duties under the
Political Reform Act. (Section 83114(b).) We do not provide
advice to third parties about another person's duties unless
the third party has been authorized to request advice by the
person whose duties are in question. (Regulation
18329(b)(8)(B).) Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the
question you have raised. However, I have referred your letter
to our Enforcement Division for review. If you have any
questions, that division can be reached at (916) 322-6441.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh
cc: Roger Brown

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

/

GRAND JURY
COUNTY GOVERNMENT CENTER
SAN LUIS OBISPO, CALIFORNIA 93408

May 6, 1988

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Howard D. Mankins - Alleged Conflict of Interest

Dear Sir:

The Grand Jury has conducted an extensive investigation of citizen complaints alleging that Howard D. Mankins, Mayor of the City of Arroyo Grande, has failed to abstain from participating and voting upon property development issues before the Arroyo Grande City Council which he knows or has reason to know he may have a financial interest.

The primary focus of our investigation has been a large-scale residential subdivision which has been designated as the Royal Oaks Estates development. Our examination of the Royal Oaks Estates has summarily revealed the following factual information:

The general partners of the Royal Oaks Estates development are Don McHaney Realty, William E. and Fleeta M. Lackey, DBA Royal Oaks Estates. Mid-Coast Land Company, Inc., has been specifically identified as a limited partner of the Royal Oak Estates. Mid-Coast Land Company, Inc. is providing the financing for the development per authority of Finance Code §751.3. Mid-Coast Land Company, Inc. is a wholly owned subsidiary of Mid-State Bank. Mayor Howard D. Mankins reports that he owns between $10,000 and $100,000 of Mid-State Bank common stock. In addition, Mankins has a Durable Power of Attorney as an individual and jointly with three family members, to administer the Leo A. Brisco Trust. We have determined that the Leo A. Brisco Trust owns in excess of $1,000,000 of Mid-State Bank common stock. Leo A. Brisco, prior to his demise, was the former Chairman of the Board of Directors for Mid-State Bank. We are informed that Howard Mankins, his immediate family and the Brisco Trust, owns approximately 5% of the available Mid-State Bank common stock.

May 6, 1988
Page Two

Our examination of the Aroyo Grande City Council minutes
reveals that Mayor Mankins has repeatedly voted in favor
of the Royal Oaks Estates development during his term of
office. At present, final grading of the development is
nearly completed and requests for building permits are
forthcoming.

In addition, we have received complaints alleging that
Mayor Mankins frequently participates in discussions of
and votes on real property issues before the council
involving properties which are contiguous to and/or in
near proximity to properties owned by Mankins, his family
and/or the Leo A. Brisco Trust. Mayor Mankins has
extensive real and commercial properties located within
the City of Arroyo Grande as evidenced by his 1988
financial disclosure statement. See attachments.
Assessor's Parcel Map 77-051 identifies the location of
properties owned by Mankins, excluding his principle
residence, which is identified by Assessor's Parcel
numbers 77-051 - 05, 06, 07, and 08. The parcel map is
attached for reference.

The City of Arroyo Grande is one of the most rapidly
developing communities in San Luis Obispo County, both
residentially and commercially. We have received
information that Mayor Mankins, as an elected city
official, is participating in negotiations with Cal-Trans
to acquire an overpass or off-ramp at southbound U.S.
Hwy. 101 at or near the Hillcrest Drive and/or Brisco Road
Streets as identified on the attached parcel map. These
discussions with Cal-Trans, et al., are reportedly
designed to provide a resolution to the near grid-lock
traffic congestion of the Oak Park overpass and ramp to
the Oak Park shopping centers. The shopping complexes are
located on the north side of U.S. Hwy. 101 opposite
Hillcrest Drive and north to Oak Park Road. El Camino
Real of old Hwy. 101, as identified on the parcel map,
parallels southbound U.S. Hwy 101.

Brisco Lumber Company, which is owned and operated by
Mayor Mankins, is located at the corner of El Camino Real
and Brisco Road, APN 77-051-17, 18, and 19. Refer to the
parcel map. Complainants are alleging that if Mayor
Mankins is successful in acquiring approval of an
over-pass and/or off-ramp in the area of either Hillcrest
Drive or Brisco Road, that the improved access will have a
foreseeable financial impact by increasing the value of
properties owned by Mankins within the aforedescribed
tract area.

May 6, 1988
Page Three

Based upon the foregoing facts and circumstances, we are
seeking a legal opinion from your agency to determine if Mayor
Howard D. Mankins is in violation of the conflict of interest
provisions of the Fair Political Practices Act. If, in your
opinion, Mayor Mankins has violated the Act, we respectfully
request the initiation of administrative action by your agency
to eliminate future violations. We would also request that
your legal opinion be communicated to the San Luis Obispo
County Grand Jury in confidence.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

C. L. Richmond, Foreman
San Luis Obispo County Grand Jury

By: R. C. Mansfield, Chief Investigator

Enclosures ·

cc. Barry T. LaBarbera, District Attorney

embedding.qa_text: **California Fair Political Practices Commission** June 1, 1988 C. L. Richmond, Foreman San Luis Obispo County Grand Jury County Government Center San Luis Obispo, CA 93408 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. G-88-201 Dear Mr. Richmond: You have written seeking an opinion concerning Arroyo Grande Mayor Howard Mankins' duties under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ The Commission's Legal Division does provide written advice to persons with questions about their duties under the Political Reform Act. (Section 83114(b).) We do not provide advice to third parties about another person's duties unless: • the third party has been authorized to request advice by the person whose duties are in question. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B).) Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the question you have raised. However, I have referred your letter to our Enforcement Division for review. If you have any questions, that division can be reached at (916) 322-6441. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel DMG:plh cc: Roger Brown 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. May 6, 1988 Fair Political Practices Commission Legal Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Howard D. Mankins - Alleged Conflict of Interest Dear Sir: The Grand Jury has conducted an extensive investigation of citizen complaints alleging that Howard D. Mankins, Mayor of the City of Arroyo Grande, has failed to abstain from participating and voting upon property development issues before the Arroyo Grande City Council which he knows or has reason to know he may have a financial interest. The primary focus of our investigation has been a large-scale residential subdivision which has been designated as the Royal Oaks Estates development. Our examination of the Royal Oaks Estates has summarily revealed the following factual information: The general partners of the Royal Oaks Estates development are Don McHaney Realty, William E. and Fleeta M. Lackey, DBA Royal Oaks Estates. Mid-Coast Land Company, Inc., has been specifically identified as a limited partner of the Royal Oak Estates. Mid-Coast Land Company, Inc. is providing the financing for the development per authority of Finance Code §751.3. Mid-Coast Land Company, Inc. is a wholly owned subsidiary of Mid-State Bank. Mayor Howard D. Mankins reports that he owns between $10,000 and $100,000 of Mid-State Bank common stock. In addition, Mankins has a Durable Power of Attorney as an individual and jointly with three family members, to administer the Leo A. Brisco Trust. We have determined that the Leo A. Brisco Trust owns in excess of $1,000,000 of Mid-State Bank common stock. Leo A. Brisco, prior to his demise, was the former Chairman of the Board of Directors for Mid-State Bank. We are informed that Howard Mankins, his immediate family and the Brisco Trust, owns approximately 5% of the available Mid-State Bank common stock. Our examination of
